codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex agreed PASS. Five adversarial questions raised: (1) negative-B GIVEN clause precision — minor, adequate as written; (2) check.pattern is only baseline_head_sha so naive grep-only validator could under-test AC2 — minor, check.expect is the normative part and explicitly names both mismatches; (3) AC1/AC3-AC6 drift — confirmed none; (4) dispatch-owned relationship clarity — adequate per Risk 3; (5) simultaneous mismatch edge case — not a blocker, implication covers it. One minor wording note: ticket SHA clause previously said 'baseline mismatch' — verified it was correctly tightened to 'baseline_head_sha mismatch' in this iteration. Codex final verdict: PASS with minor hardening suggestions, no remaining blockers.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All codex findings classified. One in_scope_minor (check.pattern narrowness) logged below as a non-blocking observation. No PROPOSED_FIX requires a spec change that would block approval — the check.expect text is the normative verifier text and it is strong. Remaining findings are out_of_scope or confirmed non-issues.

verification_summary.ac2_negative_a_sha_mismatch: worker-B baseline_head_sha='xyz999' → dev.status='blocked' citing baseline_head_sha mismatch
verification_summary.ac2_negative_b_dirty_snapshot_mismatch: worker-B baseline_dirty_snapshot=' M extra-file.md' while dispatch value is '' → dev.status='blocked' citing baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch
verification_summary.ac2_check_expect_anti_half_pass_clause: a dev implementation that blocks SHA mismatch only while ignoring dirty-snapshot mismatch does not satisfy this criterion
verification_summary.ac2_internal_consistency: no contradictions — M2 says blocked (not warned) if workers disagree on baseline values; both negative cases use dev.status='blocked'; positive case establishes the correct aggregate shape
verification_summary.drift_detection_method: byte-length comparison against git-committed prior version at 7c35b5b + git show confirmed text identity for all then+check fields of AC1/AC3-AC6

issues.finding: AC2 check.pattern field is 'baseline_head_sha' only. A grep-only verification tool running only this pattern against commands/dev.md would find baseline_head_sha text but could miss whether baseline_dirty_snapshot blocking is actually documented there. The normative enforcement is in check.expect, which explicitly requires both mismatches to be described as blocking and includes an anti-half-pass clause.
issues.proposed_fix_from_codex: Strengthen AC2 check.pattern to also include 'baseline_dirty_snapshot' or use a multi-pattern expectation requiring evidence for both mismatch types.
issues.qa_decision: Not blocking this iteration. The check.expect text is the normative part of the AC and it is unambiguous. If a future BA cycle revises the AC JSON schema to make check.pattern the primary verifier rather than check.expect, this would need addressing then.

qa.approval_notes: Iteration 3 change is correctly scoped and internally consistent. AC2 now has three logical segments: (1) positive case — aggregate carries all three provenance fields correctly; (2) negative case A — baseline_head_sha mismatch blocks; (3) negative case B (new) — baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch also blocks. Both negative cases use dev.status='blocked' with field-specific blocking_issue labels. The check.expect anti-half-pass clause explicitly disqualifies any dev implementation that handles only the SHA case. The SHA mismatch label was also tightened from the vague 'baseline mismatch' (iteration 2) to 'baseline_head_sha mismatch' (iteration 3), removing a consistency gap codex noted in prior iteration context. AC1, AC3-AC6 are byte-identical to the prior committed iteration-2 version. The spec D1/D2/D3 goals, MoSCoW requirements, edge cases, and out-of-scope observations are all consistent with the AC set. No contradictions detected. Approved for dev implementation.
